.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/entity-linker.ts:168: * @returns Array of candidate noun phrase tokens (lowercase, deduplicated).
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/entity-linker.ts:452:  // Track seen values per canonical name using Sets for O(1) dedup instead of O(n) .includes()
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:63:RRF Fusion (k=40) + Adaptive Fusion -> Unified scores
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:85:(no score changes)     (single scoring point)  (score changes: YES)    (score changes: NO)
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:99:Post-channel: constitutional memory injection, quality score filtering, tier/contextType filtering.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:108:5. Testing effect — FSRS strengthening write-back (opt-in via `trackAccess`)
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:116:Cross-encoder reranking (optional, min 2 results) followed by MPAB chunk-to-memory aggregation. Aggregation formula: `parentScore = sMax + 0.3 * Sum(rest) / sqrt(N)` where `sMax` is the best chunk score and N is the remaining chunk count. Chunk ordering preserves `chunk_index` document order (B2 guarantee). `contentSource` metadata marks provenance (`reassembled_chunks` or `file_read_fallback`).
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:119:**Score immutability invariant**: Stage 4 MUST NOT modify scores. Enforced via compile-time `Stage4ReadonlyRow` readonly fields and runtime `captureScoreSnapshot` / `verifyScoreInvariant` defence-in-depth. Applies memory-state filtering (HOT/WARM/COLD/DORMANT/ARCHIVED with per-tier limits), evidence gap detection (Z-score confidence check), quality floor (`QUALITY_FLOOR=0.005`), and token budget truncation.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:130:**Formula**: `score = Sum 1/(k + rank_i)` where k=40 (tuned for ~1000-memory corpus)
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:138:- **10% Convergence Bonus**: Results in multiple sources get +10% score boost
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:141:- **MMR Diversity**: Maximal Marginal Relevance reranking reduces near-duplicate results
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:142:- **Recency Boost**: Time-aware score adjustment favoring recently updated memories
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:143:- **Co-activation Boost**: Graph-neighbor score propagation via 2-hop causal traversal
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:148:// RRF score = 1/(40+2) + 1/(40+5) + 1.5/(40+1)
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:157:score(D, Q) = Sum IDF(qi) * (tf(qi,D) * (k1+1)) / (tf(qi,D) + k1 * (1-b + b*|D|/avgdl))
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:231:**Trade-off**: Adds 200-500ms latency but improves precision by 15-25%.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:250:| **TypeScript**       | `channel-representation.ts`, `channel-enforcement.ts`, `confidence-truncation.ts` (quality)   |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:297:| `causal-boost.ts`          | -      | TypeScript | Causal-neighbor score boosting for graph traversal  |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:298:| `session-boost.ts`         | -      | TypeScript | Session-attention score boosting                    |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:305:| `confidence-truncation.ts` | -      | TypeScript | Removes low-confidence tail using 2x median gap heuristic (min 3 results) (Sprint 3) |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:309:| `evidence-gap-detector.ts` | -      | TypeScript | Z-score confidence check on RRF scores to detect low-confidence retrieval |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:310:| `fsrs.ts`                  | -      | TypeScript | Temporal-structural coherence: FSRS stability augmented with graph centrality |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:322:| `validation-metadata.ts`   | -      | TypeScript | Validation signal scoring multiplier for retrieval results |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:333:| `confidence-scoring.ts`    | -      | TypeScript | Computes calibrated confidence scores for retrieval results |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:383:   MMR diversity reranking -> Reduce near-duplicate results
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:384:   Co-activation boost -> Graph-neighbor score propagation
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:385:   Recency boost -> Time-aware score adjustment
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:452:| v4      | FSRS columns (`stability`, `difficulty`, `last_review`, `review_count`) + `memory_conflicts` table + FSRS indexes |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:455:| v7      | `partial` embedding_status + `idx_embedding_pending` + `idx_fts_fallback` (REQ-031, T096) |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:491:// Preserves code identifiers (underscores), removes punctuation
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:510:// -> RRF + Adaptive Fusion -> MMR diversity -> Sorted by combined score
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:541:**Fallback**: `understand` intent if no match (score < 3.0).
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:561:// Track P95 latency (last 100 searches)
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:656:Top-ranked results seed a 2-hop BFS traversal over causal edges. Discovered neighbors receive a score boost with `SEED_FRACTION=0.25` strength. A `1/sqrt(neighbor_count)` fan-effect divisor (R17) prevents hub nodes from appearing in every co-activation result.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:659:Tracks degree change over a 7-day rolling window using the `degree_snapshots` table. Momentum = currentDegree - pastDegree. Nodes gaining connections receive a score bonus: `clamp(momentum * 0.01, 0, 0.05)` — additive cap of +0.05.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:668:Measures graph density and reports metrics used for R10 entity extraction escalation decisions.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:678:**TM-02: Same-Path and Hash Dedup Hardening** (`save/dedup.ts`):
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:679:Same-path `unchanged` only applies to healthy existing rows (`success`, `pending`, `partial`), so unhealthy embedding states still re-enter indexing. Cross-path content-hash dedup accepts chunked parents only when the parent row is in valid `partial` state and ignores invalid parent rows marked `complete`.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:682:3-layer pre-storage validation gated via `SPECKIT_SAVE_QUALITY_GATE` (default ON, graduated Sprint 4):
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:688:| 3. Semantic Dedup | Cosine similarity against existing memories | Threshold: 0.92 rejects near-duplicates |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:690:Signal density threshold: **0.4** — below this, content quality is too low. 14-day warn-only period (MR12 mitigation): logs scores but does not block saves during ramp-up.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:704:Scores chunks by anchor presence + content density. Composite score: `anchorScore * ANCHOR_WEIGHT + densityScore * DENSITY_WEIGHT`. Chunks below the `DEFAULT_THINNING_THRESHOLD=0.3` are dropped before indexing.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:724:**Scan Invalidation Hooks** (`handlers/memory-index.ts` / `handlers/mutation-hooks.ts`):
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:725:`memory_index_scan` now runs the broader post-mutation invalidation hook behavior whenever a scan indexes, updates, or stale-deletes rows.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:731:Min-max normalization maps composite scores to [0,1], fixing 15:1 magnitude mismatches between scoring dimensions. Gated via `SPECKIT_SCORE_NORMALIZATION` (default ON, graduated Sprint 4). Single-result sets normalize to 1.0; equal scores normalize to 1.0.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:736:**Degree Cache Invalidation** (`graph-search-fn.ts`):
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:740:FSRS stability is adjusted by a 2D multiplier matrix of context type x importance tier:
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:751:`wasUseful=false` validations apply a demotion multiplier to composite scores. Penalty: -0.1 per negative validation, floor at 0.3 (never suppress below 30%). 30-day half-life recovery: penalty halves over time if no further negative feedback. Gated via `SPECKIT_NEGATIVE_FEEDBACK` (default ON).
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:754:Validation-count-based tier promotion engine. Thresholds: **5** positive validations = normal -> important, **10** = important -> critical. Throttle safeguard: max **3** promotions per **8-hour** rolling window. `NON_PROMOTABLE_TIERS`: critical, constitutional, temporary, deprecated. Promotion audit logged to `memory_promotion_audit` table.

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:877:- Quality floor corrected from 0.2 to 0.005 (D3) — aligns with RRF score range

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md:18:A four-stage retrieval pipeline that takes a search query through candidate generation, score fusion, reranking and final filtering to produce ranked memory results.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md:47:| `stage2-fusion.ts` | Stage 2: Fusion + Signal Integration. The single authoritative scoring point. Applies 9 signal steps in fixed order: session boost, causal boost, co-activation spreading, community co-retrieval, graph signals, FSRS testing effect, intent weights (non-hybrid only, G2 prevention), artifact routing, feedback signals, artifact limiting, anchor metadata and validation metadata scoring. |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md:49:| `stage4-filter.ts` | Stage 4: Filter + Annotate. Removes results below minimum memory state priority, enforces per-tier limits, runs TRM evidence-gap detection and attaches annotation metadata. Enforces the score immutability invariant at runtime. |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md:50:| `types.ts` | All pipeline data contracts: `PipelineRow`, `Stage4ReadonlyRow`, `PipelineConfig`, stage I/O interfaces (`Stage1Input`/`Output` through `Stage4Input`/`Output`), `PipelineResult`, `ScoreSnapshot` and the `resolveEffectiveScore()` shared score resolution function. Also provides `captureScoreSnapshot()` and `verifyScoreInvariant()` for Stage 4 defence-in-depth. |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md:65:      |  artifact routing, feedback signals, FSRS write-back
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md:73:  PipelineResult { results, metadata, annotations, trace }
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md:87:- FSRS testing effect fires only when `trackAccess` is explicitly true.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md:93:- Preserves Stage 2 scores as `stage2Score` for auditability.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md:98:- TRM evidence-gap detection (Z-score confidence check on score distribution).
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md:99:- Runtime score invariant verification via snapshot comparison.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md:105:1. **Single Scoring Point.** All score modifications happen in Stage 2 (fusion) or Stage 3 (reranking). Stage 4 must not change any score field.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/pipeline/README.md:108:4. **Score Resolution Consistency.** All stages use the shared `resolveEffectiveScore()` function from `types.ts` with fallback chain: `intentAdjustedScore` > `rrfScore` > `score` > `similarity/100`, clamped to [0, 1].

   178	
   179	function createTelemetry(): RetrievalTelemetry {
   180	  const roadmapDefaults = getMemoryRoadmapDefaults();
   181	
   182	  return {
   183	    enabled: isExtendedTelemetryEnabled(),
   184	    timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
   185	    latency: {
   186	      totalLatencyMs: 0,
   187	      candidateLatencyMs: 0,
   188	      fusionLatencyMs: 0,
   189	      rerankLatencyMs: 0,
   190	      boostLatencyMs: 0,
   191	    },
   192	    mode: {
   193	      selectedMode: null,
   194	      modeOverrideApplied: false,
   195	      pressureLevel: null,
   196	    },
   197	    fallback: {
   198	      fallbackTriggered: false,
   199	      degradedModeActive: false,
   200	    },
   201	    quality: {
   202	      resultCount: 0,
   203	      avgRelevanceScore: 0,
   204	      topResultScore: 0,
   205	      boostImpactDelta: 0,
   206	      extractionCountInSession: 0,
   207	      qualityProxyScore: 0,
   208	    },
   209	    architecture: {
   210	      phase: roadmapDefaults.phase,
   211	      capabilities: { ...roadmapDefaults.capabilities },
   212	      scopeDimensionsTracked: roadmapDefaults.scopeDimensionsTracked,
   213	    },
   214	    graphHealth: {
   215	      killSwitchActive: false,
   216	      causalBoosted: 0,

   220	      totalGraphInjected: 0,

   231	
   232	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   233	   4. RECORDING FUNCTIONS
   234	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   235	
   236	function recordLatency(t: RetrievalTelemetry, stage: LatencyStage, ms: number): void {
   237	  if (!t.enabled) return;
   238	  const clamped = Math.max(0, Number.isFinite(ms) ? ms : 0);
   239	  t.latency[stage] = clamped;
   240	  // Recompute total from components
   241	  t.latency.totalLatencyMs =
   242	    t.latency.candidateLatencyMs +
   243	    t.latency.fusionLatencyMs +
   244	    t.latency.rerankLatencyMs +
   245	    t.latency.boostLatencyMs;
   246	}
   247	
   248	function recordMode(
   249	  t: RetrievalTelemetry,
   250	  mode: string,
   251	  override: boolean,
   252	  pressure: string | null,
   253	  tokenUsageRatio?: number,
   254	): void {
   255	  if (!t.enabled) return;
   256	  t.mode.selectedMode = mode;
   257	  t.mode.modeOverrideApplied = override;
   258	  t.mode.pressureLevel = pressure;
   259	  if (typeof tokenUsageRatio === 'number' && Number.isFinite(tokenUsageRatio)) {
   260	    t.mode.tokenUsageRatio = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, tokenUsageRatio));
   261	  }
   262	}
   263	
   264	function recordFallback(t: RetrievalTelemetry, reason?: string): void {
   265	  if (!t.enabled) return;
   266	  t.fallback.fallbackTriggered = true;
   267	  if (reason) {
   268	    t.fallback.fallbackReason = reason;
   269	  }
   270	  t.fallback.degradedModeActive = true;
   271	}

     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Context Metrics
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	// Phase 023: Lightweight session quality tracking.
     5	// Collects events during MCP tool dispatch and computes quality scores.
     6	// In-memory only — no DB persistence needed for now.
     7	
     8	import { getStats as getGraphStats } from '../code-graph/code-graph-db.js';
     9	
    10	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    11	   1. TYPES
    12	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    13	
    14	export interface SessionMetrics {
    15	  sessionId: string;
    16	  startedAt: string;
    17	  lastToolCallAt: string | null;
    18	  toolCallCount: number;
    19	  memoryRecoveryCalls: number;
    20	  codeGraphQueries: number;
    21	  specFolderTransitions: number;
    22	  currentSpecFolder: string | null;
    23	  primed: boolean;
    24	}
    25	
    26	export type QualityLevel = 'healthy' | 'degraded' | 'critical';
    27	
    28	export interface QualityScore {
    29	  level: QualityLevel;
    30	  score: number; // 0.0 – 1.0
    31	  factors: {
    32	    recency: number;        // 1.0 if recent tool call, decays over time
    33	    recovery: number;       // 1.0 if memory recovered this session
    34	    graphFreshness: number; // 1.0 fresh, 0.5 stale, 0.0 empty
    35	    continuity: number;     // 1.0 if spec folder stable, lower on transitions
    36	  };
    37	}
    38	
    39	export type MetricEventKind =
    40	  | 'tool_call'
    41	  | 'memory_recovery'
    42	  | 'code_graph_query'
    43	  | 'spec_folder_change'
    44	  | 'bootstrap';
    45	
    46	export type BootstrapSource = 'hook' | 'mcp_auto' | 'agent' | 'manual' | 'tool';
    47	export type BootstrapCompleteness = 'full' | 'partial' | 'minimal';
    48	
    49	export interface BootstrapRecord {
    50	  source: BootstrapSource;
    51	  durationMs: number;
    52	  completeness: BootstrapCompleteness;
    53	  timestamp: string;
    54	}
    55	
    56	export interface MetricEvent {
    57	  kind: MetricEventKind;
    58	  toolName?: string;
    59	  specFolder?: string;
    60	}
    61	
    62	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    63	   2. STATE
    64	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    65	
    66	const sessionId = `sess_${Date.now().toString(36)}_${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 8)}`;
    67	const startedAt = new Date().toISOString();
    68	
    69	let lastToolCallAt: number | null = null;
    70	let toolCallCount = 0;
    71	let memoryRecoveryCalls = 0;
    72	let codeGraphQueries = 0;
    73	let specFolderTransitions = 0;
    74	let currentSpecFolder: string | null = null;
    75	let primed = false;
    76	const bootstrapRecords: BootstrapRecord[] = [];
    77	
    78	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    79	   3. CONSTANTS
    80	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    81	
    82	const FIVE_MINUTES_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000;
    83	const SIXTY_MINUTES_MS = 60 * 60 * 1000;
    84	// Matches the session-snapshot graph staleness threshold.
    85	const GRAPH_FRESHNESS_THRESHOLD_MS = 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000; // 24 hours
    86	
    87	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    88	   4. EVENT RECORDING
    89	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    90	
    91	/** Record a metric event from tool dispatch or lifecycle hooks. */
    92	export function recordMetricEvent(event: MetricEvent): void {
    93	  switch (event.kind) {
    94	    case 'tool_call':
    95	      toolCallCount++;
    96	      lastToolCallAt = Date.now();
    97	      if (!primed) primed = true;
    98	      break;
    99	
   100	    case 'memory_recovery':
   101	      memoryRecoveryCalls++;
   102	      break;
   103	
   104	    case 'code_graph_query':
   105	      codeGraphQueries++;
   106	      break;
   107	
   108	    case 'spec_folder_change':
   109	      if (event.specFolder && event.specFolder !== currentSpecFolder) {
   110	        specFolderTransitions++;
   111	        currentSpecFolder = event.specFolder;
   112	      }
   113	      break;
   114	
   115	    case 'bootstrap':
   116	      // Handled by recordBootstrapEvent — no-op here
   117	      break;
   118	  }
   119	}
   120	
   121	/** Phase 024 / Item 9: Record a bootstrap telemetry event. */
   122	export function recordBootstrapEvent(
   123	  source: BootstrapSource,
   124	  durationMs: number,
   125	  completeness: BootstrapCompleteness,
   126	): void {
   127	  bootstrapRecords.push({
   128	    source,
   129	    durationMs,
   130	    completeness,
   131	    timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
   132	  });
   133	}
   134	
   135	/** Get all bootstrap records for diagnostics. */
   136	export function getBootstrapRecords(): readonly BootstrapRecord[] {
   137	  return bootstrapRecords;
   138	}
   139	
   140	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   141	   5. METRICS SNAPSHOT
   142	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   143	
   144	/** Return a read-only snapshot of current session metrics. */
   145	export function getSessionMetrics(): SessionMetrics {
   146	  return {
   147	    sessionId,
   148	    startedAt,
   149	    lastToolCallAt: lastToolCallAt ? new Date(lastToolCallAt).toISOString() : null,
   150	    toolCallCount,
   151	    memoryRecoveryCalls,
   152	    codeGraphQueries,
   153	    specFolderTransitions,
   154	    currentSpecFolder,
   155	    primed,
   156	  };
   157	}
   158	
   159	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   160	   6. QUALITY SCORING
   161	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   162	
   163	/** Compute recency factor: 1.0 if <5 min, linear decay to 0.0 at 60 min. */
   164	function computeRecency(): number {
   165	  if (lastToolCallAt === null) return 0;
   166	  const elapsed = Date.now() - lastToolCallAt;
   167	  if (elapsed <= FIVE_MINUTES_MS) return 1.0;
   168	  if (elapsed >= SIXTY_MINUTES_MS) return 0.0;
   169	  // Linear decay between 5 min and 60 min
   170	  return 1.0 - (elapsed - FIVE_MINUTES_MS) / (SIXTY_MINUTES_MS - FIVE_MINUTES_MS);
   171	}
   172	
   173	/** Compute recovery factor: 1.0 if any memory recovery call, 0.0 otherwise. */
   174	function computeRecovery(): number {
   175	  return memoryRecoveryCalls > 0 ? 1.0 : 0.0;
   176	}
   177	
   178	/** Compute graph freshness: 1.0 fresh, 0.5 stale, 0.0 empty/error. */
   179	function computeGraphFreshness(): number {
   180	  try {
   181	    const stats = getGraphStats();
   182	    if (stats.totalFiles === 0) return 0.0;
   183	    if (!stats.lastScanTimestamp) return 0.5;
   184	    const age = Date.now() - new Date(stats.lastScanTimestamp).getTime();
   185	    return age <= GRAPH_FRESHNESS_THRESHOLD_MS ? 1.0 : 0.5;
   186	  } catch {
   187	    return 0.0;
   188	  }
   189	}
   190	
   191	/** Compute continuity: 1.0 if 0-1 transitions, 0.5 if 2-3, 0.0 if >3. */
   192	function computeContinuity(): number {
   193	  if (specFolderTransitions <= 1) return 1.0;
   194	  if (specFolderTransitions <= 3) return 0.5;
   195	  return 0.0;
   196	}
   197	
   198	/**
   199	 * F047: Single source of truth for lastToolCallAt timestamp.
   200	 * session-health.ts should use this instead of the memory-surface duplicate.
   201	 */
   202	export function getLastToolCallAt(): number | null {
   203	  return lastToolCallAt;
   204	}
   205	
   206	/** Compute overall quality score and level. */
   207	export function computeQualityScore(): QualityScore {
   208	  const factors = {
   209	    recency: computeRecency(),
   210	    recovery: computeRecovery(),
   211	    graphFreshness: computeGraphFreshness(),
   212	    continuity: computeContinuity(),
   213	  };
   214	
   215	  // F065: Weight rationale for quality score factors:
   216	  //
   217	  //   recency (0.35)       — Highest weight because stale sessions are the primary
   218	  //                          degradation signal. A 60-min gap almost certainly means
   219	  //                          the LLM's working context has drifted from the codebase.
   220	  //
   221	  //   continuity (0.25)    — Second-highest. Frequent spec folder switches indicate
   222	  //                          context fragmentation; stable focus correlates with quality.
   223	  //
   224	  //   recovery (0.20)      — A memory_context({ mode: "resume" }) call is the most
   225	  //                          reliable signal that the session has recovered prior state.
   226	  //                          Binary (done/not-done) so lower weight than continuous factors.
   227	  //
   228	  //   graphFreshness (0.20) — A stale code graph means structural queries return
   229	  //                          outdated symbols. Tied with recovery because both are
   230	  //                          "enabling" factors rather than direct quality signals.
   231	  //
   232	  //   Total: 0.35 + 0.25 + 0.20 + 0.20 = 1.00
   233	  const score = (
   234	    factors.recency * 0.35 +
   235	    factors.recovery * 0.20 +
   236	    factors.graphFreshness * 0.20 +
   237	    factors.continuity * 0.25
   238	  );
   239	
   240	  let level: QualityLevel;

    32	
    33	/** Strip absolute paths, stack traces, and truncate for safe user-facing hints. */
    34	function sanitizeErrorForHint(msg: string): string {
    35	  return msg
    36	    .replace(/(^|[\s(])\/(?:[^/\n]+\/)*[^:\n)"'\]]+/g, (_match, prefix: string) => `${prefix}[path]`)
    37	    .replace(/(^|[\s(])[A-Za-z]:\\(?:[^\\\n]+\\)*[^:\n)"'\]]+/g, (_match, prefix: string) => `${prefix}[path]`)
    38	    .replace(/^[ \t]*at .+$/gm, '')            // strip stack trace lines
    39	    .replace(/\n{2,}/g, '\n')                   // collapse blank lines left by stripping
    40	    .trim()
    41	    .slice(0, 200);
    42	}
    43	
    44	/** Redact absolute paths: keep only project-relative portion or basename. */
    45	function redactPath(absolutePath: string): string {
    46	  const normalizedPath = toNormalizedPath(absolutePath);
    47	  if (normalizedPath.startsWith('.opencode/')) return normalizedPath;
    48	  if (normalizedPath.startsWith('specs/')) return normalizedPath;
    49	  const opencodeIdx = normalizedPath.indexOf('/.opencode/');
    50	  const specsIdx = normalizedPath.indexOf('/specs/');
    51	  if (opencodeIdx !== -1) return normalizedPath.slice(opencodeIdx + 1);
    52	  if (specsIdx !== -1) return normalizedPath.slice(specsIdx + 1);
    53	  // Fallback: basename only
    54	  const lastSlash = normalizedPath.lastIndexOf('/');
    55	  return lastSlash !== -1 ? normalizedPath.slice(lastSlash + 1) : normalizedPath;
    56	}
    57	
    58	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    59	   CONSTANTS
    60	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    61	
    62	// Read version from package.json at module load time using ESM-relative paths.
    63	// WHY try-catch: if package.json is missing or malformed, the server should still start
    64	const SERVER_VERSION: string = (() => {
    65	  const packageCandidates = [
    66	    resolve(import.meta.dirname, '../package.json'),
    67	    resolve(import.meta.dirname, '../../package.json'),
    68	  ];
    69	
    70	  try {
    71	    for (const candidate of packageCandidates) {
    72	      if (!existsSync(candidate)) {
    73	        continue;
    74	      }
    75	      const pkg = JSON.parse(readFileSync(candidate, 'utf-8'));
    76	      if (pkg?.version) {
    77	        return pkg.version;
    78	      }
    79	    }
    80	    return 'unknown';
    81	  } catch (_error: unknown) {
    82	    return 'unknown';
    83	  }
    84	})();
    85	
    86	const DIVERGENT_ALIAS_REPORT_MODE = 'divergent_aliases';
    87	const DEFAULT_DIVERGENT_ALIAS_LIMIT = 20;
    88	const MAX_DIVERGENT_ALIAS_LIMIT = 200;
    89	const DOT_OPENCODE_SPECS_SEGMENT = '/.opencode/specs/';
    90	const SPECS_SEGMENT = '/specs/';
    91	
    92	interface AliasConflictDbRow {
    93	  file_path: string;
    94	  content_hash: string | null;
    95	  spec_folder?: string | null;
    96	}
    97	
    98	interface DivergentAliasVariant {
    99	  filePath: string;
   100	  contentHash: string | null;
   101	}
   102	
   103	interface DivergentAliasGroup {
   104	  normalizedPath: string;
   105	  specFolders: string[];
   106	  distinctHashCount: number;
   107	  variants: DivergentAliasVariant[];
   108	}
   109	
   110	interface DivergentAliasBucket {
   111	  hasDotOpencodeVariant: boolean;
   112	  hasSpecsVariant: boolean;
   113	  variants: Map<string, string | null>;
   114	  hashes: Set<string>;
   115	  specFolders: Set<string>;
   116	}
   117	
   118	function toNormalizedPath(filePath: string): string {
   119	  return filePath.replace(/\\/g, '/');
   120	}
   121	
   122	function toSpecAliasKey(filePath: string): string {
   123	  const normalizedPath = toNormalizedPath(filePath);
   124	  return normalizedPath
   125	    .replace(/\/\.opencode\/specs\//g, '/specs/')
   126	    .replace(/^\.opencode\/specs\//, 'specs/');
   127	}
   128	
   129	function isSpecsAliasPath(filePath: string): boolean {
   130	  const normalizedPath = toNormalizedPath(filePath);
   131	  return (
   132	    normalizedPath.includes(DOT_OPENCODE_SPECS_SEGMENT) ||
   133	    normalizedPath.includes(SPECS_SEGMENT) ||
   134	    normalizedPath.startsWith('.opencode/specs/') ||
   135	    normalizedPath.startsWith('specs/')
   136	  );
   137	}
   138	
   139	function isDotOpencodeVariantPath(normalizedPath: string): boolean {
   140	  return normalizedPath.includes(DOT_OPENCODE_SPECS_SEGMENT) || normalizedPath.startsWith('.opencode/specs/');
   141	}
   142	
   143	function isSpecsVariantPath(normalizedPath: string): boolean {
   144	  if (isDotOpencodeVariantPath(normalizedPath)) {
   145	    return false;
   146	  }
   147	  return normalizedPath.includes(SPECS_SEGMENT) || normalizedPath.startsWith('specs/');
   148	}
   149	
   150	function getDivergentAliasGroups(rows: AliasConflictDbRow[], limit: number): DivergentAliasGroup[] {
   151	  if (!rows.length) {
   152	    return [];
   153	  }
   154	
   155	  const buckets = new Map<string, DivergentAliasBucket>();
   156	
   157	  for (const row of rows) {
   158	    if (!row || typeof row.file_path !== 'string' || row.file_path.length === 0) {
   159	      continue;
   160	    }
   161	
   162	    const normalizedPath = toNormalizedPath(row.file_path);
   163	    const aliasKey = toSpecAliasKey(normalizedPath);
   164	    let bucket = buckets.get(aliasKey);
   165	    if (!bucket) {
   166	      bucket = {
   167	        hasDotOpencodeVariant: false,
   168	        hasSpecsVariant: false,
   169	        variants: new Map<string, string | null>(),
   170	        hashes: new Set<string>(),
   171	        specFolders: new Set<string>(),
   172	      };
   173	      buckets.set(aliasKey, bucket);
   174	    }
   175	
   176	    if (isDotOpencodeVariantPath(normalizedPath)) {
   177	      bucket.hasDotOpencodeVariant = true;
   178	    }
   179	    if (isSpecsVariantPath(normalizedPath)) {
   180	      bucket.hasSpecsVariant = true;
   181	    }
   182	
   183	    bucket.variants.set(normalizedPath, row.content_hash ?? null);
   184	    if (typeof row.content_hash === 'string' && row.content_hash.trim().length > 0) {
   185	      bucket.hashes.add(row.content_hash.trim());
   186	    }
   187	    if (typeof row.spec_folder === 'string' && row.spec_folder.length > 0) {
   188	      bucket.specFolders.add(row.spec_folder);
   189	    }
   190	  }
   191	
   192	  const groups: DivergentAliasGroup[] = [];
   193	  for (const [normalizedPath, bucket] of buckets.entries()) {
   194	    if (!bucket.hasDotOpencodeVariant || !bucket.hasSpecsVariant) {
   195	      continue;
   196	    }
   197	    if (bucket.variants.size < 2 || bucket.hashes.size < 2) {
   198	      continue;
   199	    }
   200	
   201	    const variants: DivergentAliasVariant[] = Array.from(bucket.variants.entries())
   202	      .sort(([pathA], [pathB]) => pathA.localeCompare(pathB))
   203	      .map(([filePath, contentHash]) => ({ filePath: redactPath(filePath), contentHash }));
   204	
   205	    groups.push({
   206	      normalizedPath: redactPath(normalizedPath),
   207	      // Fix F21 — redact specFolders to prevent path disclosure.
   208	      specFolders: Array.from(bucket.specFolders).sort().map(sf => redactPath(sf)),
   209	      distinctHashCount: bucket.hashes.size,
   210	      variants,
   211	    });
   212	  }
   213	
   214	  groups.sort((a, b) => a.normalizedPath.localeCompare(b.normalizedPath));
   215	  return groups.slice(0, limit);
   216	}

   222	/** Handle memory_health tool -- returns system health status and diagnostics. */
   223	async function handleMemoryHealth(args: HealthArgs): Promise<MCPResponse> {
   224	  const startTime = Date.now();

   226	  const requestId = randomUUID();

   228	    await checkDatabaseUpdated();
   229	  } catch (dbStateErr: unknown) {
   230	    const message = toErrorMessage(dbStateErr);
   231	    console.error(`[memory-health] Database refresh failed [requestId=${requestId}]: ${message}`);
   232	    return createMCPErrorResponse({
   233	      tool: 'memory_health',
   234	      error: 'Database refresh failed before diagnostics completed. Retry the request or restart the MCP server.',
   235	      code: 'E021',
   236	      details: { requestId },
   237	      startTime,
   238	    });
   239	  }
   240	
   241	  const {
   242	    reportMode = 'full',
   243	    limit: rawLimit = DEFAULT_DIVERGENT_ALIAS_LIMIT,
   244	    specFolder,
   245	    autoRepair = false,
   246	    confirmed = false,
   247	  } = args ?? {};
   248	
   249	  if (reportMode !== 'full' && reportMode !== DIVERGENT_ALIAS_REPORT_MODE) {
   250	    return createMCPErrorResponse({
   251	      tool: 'memory_health',
   252	      error: `Invalid reportMode: ${String(reportMode)}. Expected "full" or "${DIVERGENT_ALIAS_REPORT_MODE}"`,
   253	      code: 'E_INVALID_INPUT',
   254	      details: { requestId },
   255	      startTime,
   256	    });
   257	  }
   258	  if (specFolder !== undefined && typeof specFolder !== 'string') {
   259	    return createMCPErrorResponse({
   260	      tool: 'memory_health',
   261	      error: 'specFolder must be a string',
   262	      code: 'E_INVALID_INPUT',
   263	      details: { requestId },
   264	      startTime,
   265	    });
   266	  }
   267	  if (typeof autoRepair !== 'boolean') {
   268	    return createMCPErrorResponse({
   269	      tool: 'memory_health',
   270	      error: 'autoRepair must be a boolean',
   271	      code: 'E_INVALID_INPUT',
   272	      details: { requestId },
   273	      startTime,
   274	    });
   275	  }
   276	  if (typeof confirmed !== 'boolean') {
   277	    return createMCPErrorResponse({
   278	      tool: 'memory_health',
   279	      error: 'confirmed must be a boolean',
   280	      code: 'E_INVALID_INPUT',

    13	
    14	# $refine TIDD-EC Prompt: 004-opencode-mnemosyne-main
    15	
    16	## 2. Role
    17	
    18	You are a research specialist in hybrid search architectures (BM25 + vector embeddings), OpenCode plugin design, memory scoping patterns, and session compaction resilience. Work like a systems analyst who can separate TypeScript wrapper behavior from backend README claims, trace plugin lifecycle and hook wiring precisely, and translate OpenCode Mnemosyne's concrete design into practical improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`.
    19	
    20	## 3. Task
    21	
    22	Research OpenCode Mnemosyne's hybrid search architecture, plugin design patterns, and compaction survival strategies to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around combining text and vector search, OpenCode plugin integration, project-vs-global memory scoping, and preserving memory awareness across context compaction. Focus on what the external repo actually proves in `src/index.ts` and what the README describes about the Mnemosyne backend. Classify each recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
    23	
    24	## 4. Context
    25	
    26	### 4.1 System Description
    27	
    28	OpenCode Mnemosyne is an OpenCode plugin that wraps the Go-based Mnemosyne local memory backend. The external repo is intentionally thin: `src/index.ts` contains the plugin entry point, CLI bridge, tool registration, scope initialization, and the `experimental.session.compacting` hook, while `README.md` describes the backend's hybrid retrieval model and intended agent workflow. The documented retrieval stack combines full-text search (SQLite FTS5 with BM25 ranking) and vector search (sqlite-vec cosine similarity using `snowflake-arctic-embed-m-v1.5`), then fuses both with Reciprocal Rank Fusion. All inference is local, the ML model is downloaded on first use (~500 MB), and no cloud APIs are involved.
    29	
    30	The plugin exposes five memory tools: `memory_recall`, `memory_recall_global`, `memory_store`, `memory_store_global`, and `memory_delete`. Project scope is derived from the current directory name and auto-initialized during plugin load; the global collection is initialized lazily on first global write. The wrapper shells out to the `mnemosyne` binary via `Bun.spawn`, quotes search strings to avoid FTS syntax issues, and injects compact memory-usage guidance into the compaction prompt so agents retain memory awareness after context resets.
    31	
    32	### 4.2 Cross-Phase Awareness Table
    33	
    34	| Phase | System | Core Pattern | Overlap Risk | Differentiation |
    35	|-------|--------|-------------|-------------|-----------------|
    36	| 001 | Engram | MCP memory server (Go, SQLite+FTS5) | 004 (FTS5 search) | Focus tool profiles, session lifecycle, topic keys |
    37	| 002 | Mex | Markdown scaffold + drift detection | 004 (memory) | Focus drift detection, scaffold, no-DB approach |
    38	| 003 | Modus Memory | FSRS spaced repetition + BM25 | 004 (BM25 search) | Focus FSRS decay, cross-referencing, query expansion |
    39	| 004 | OpenCode Mnemosyne | Hybrid search (FTS5 + vector) | 001 (FTS5), 003 (BM25) | Focus vector search, plugin architecture, scoping |
    40	
    41	### 4.3 What This Repo Already Has
    42	
    43	`Code_Environment/Public` already has Spec Kit Memory with a hybrid vector/BM25 memory retrieval pipeline in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts`, startup and resume flows in `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts`, and CocoIndex for semantic code search through `.opencode/skill/mcp-coco-index/README.md`. It also already has a compaction-oriented transport plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js` that injects structural context during `experimental.session.compacting`.
    44	
    45	What this repo does **not** currently have is an OpenCode memory plugin that wraps a standalone local binary, a first-class project/global tool pair with Mnemosyne's lightweight ergonomics, or a memory-specific compaction hook that injects tool-usage guidance directly into the compaction prompt. Do not repeat the outdated claim that Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval; compare Mnemosyne against the current code, not stale assumptions.
    46	
    47	## 5. Instructions
    48	
    49	1. Treat `/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main` as the pre-approved phase folder. Skip Gate 3, keep all writes inside this phase folder, and treat everything under `external/` as read-only.
    50	2. Start with the plugin entry point: read `external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/src/index.ts` from top to bottom before the README. Confirm how the OpenCode plugin is declared, how `ctx.directory` / `ctx.worktree` are resolved, how project names are normalized, and where collection initialization happens.
    51	3. Read `external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/package.json` immediately after `src/index.ts` to confirm packaging boundaries, plugin metadata, runtime assumptions, and whether the repo exposes any source beyond the wrapper.
    52	4. Trace the shared `mnemosyne(...args)` bridge in `src/index.ts`: `Bun.spawn`, `cwd` selection, stdout/stderr handling, exit-code failure behavior, missing-binary fallback, and the security implication of passing argv arrays instead of interpolated shell strings.
    53	5. Examine `memory_recall` and `memory_recall_global` in detail. Trace argument schemas, quoted search-string handling, project vs global flags, `--format plain`, and how the wrapper surfaces "No memories found" versus real backend output.
    54	6. Examine `memory_store` and `memory_store_global` in detail. Trace write semantics, lazy creation of the global collection, project collection auto-init, and what these choices imply for memory scoping, onboarding friction, and failure modes.
    55	7. Examine `memory_delete` in detail. Trace how document IDs are expected to flow from recall results into deletion, and what this says about memory lifecycle and hygiene expectations.
    56	8. Examine the `experimental.session.compacting` hook implementation in `src/index.ts` line by line. Treat this as the most novel feature of the phase: capture exactly what guidance is injected, when it runs, and how that compares with Public's structural compaction plugin in `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js`.
    57	9. Only after the wrapper analysis, read `external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/README.md`.     60	12. Resolve cross-phase boundaries explicitly. Do not redo phase `001` as generic FTS5 study or phase `003` as generic BM25 study. This phase owns wrapper-to-backend boundaries, vector-search implications, OpenCode plugin ergonomics, project/global scoping, AGENTS guidance, and memory-awareness compaction behavior.

    68	    ```
    69	15. Save all outputs inside this phase folder, especially under `research/`. Every meaningful finding must cite exact file paths, say whether the evidence is wrapper-confirmed or README-level, explain why it matters for `Code_Environment/Public`, classify the recommendation as `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`, and identify the affected subsystem. When research is complete, update `checklist.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, and save memory with:
    70	    ```bash
    71	    cd /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public && node .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js "/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main"
    72	    ```
    73	
    74	## 6. Research Questions
    75	
    76	1. How is hybrid retrieval presented at the plugin boundary: what does the README claim about BM25, vector similarity, and Reciprocal Rank Fusion, and what parts of that pipeline are actually visible from the wrapper?
    77	2. Why does Mnemosyne use `snowflake-arctic-embed-m-v1.5`, and what should `Code_Environment/Public` learn from that model choice even if Public already has other embedding-backed retrieval systems?
    78	3. How effective is Mnemosyne's project-vs-global scope split, and would a similar ergonomic separation improve Public's memory-save and recall flows without duplicating existing scoped retrieval features?
    79	4. What exactly does the compaction hook preserve, and how does prompt-level tool guidance differ from Public's current structural-context compaction transport?
    80	5. What operational value comes from the plugin auto-initializing the project collection and lazily initializing the global collection, and what failure or ambiguity risks come with deriving the project key from the directory basename?
    81	6. How should the README's AGENTS.md recommendation be interpreted as workflow guidance: is it merely usage advice, or does it reveal a reusable memory-discipline pattern for Public sessions?
    82	7. Compared with CocoIndex's semantic code search, what is unique about Mnemosyne's hybrid memory retrieval story, and where should those systems stay separate rather than merged?
    83	8. Compared with Public's current `memory_search` pipeline, which Mnemosyne ideas are already covered, which are partial overlaps, and which are genuinely new?
    84	9. How does the OpenCode plugin lifecycle shape adoption compared with MCP-server-based memory systems: installation, runtime assumptions, binary dependency management, and tool exposure ergonomics?
    85	10. What are the practical implications of the offline-first design, including local model download cost, first-run latency, privacy benefits, and failure handling when the binary or model is missing?
    86	
    87	## 7. Do's
    88	
    89	- Do trace the full wrapper lifecycle in `src/index.ts`, not just the README summary.
    90	- Do separate wrapper-confirmed behavior from backend claims documented only in `README.md`.
    91	- Do trace the hybrid-search narrative end to end as a contract: query entry, quoted search handoff, backend search call, and fused-ranking claims.
    92	- Do study project and global scoping as product decisions, not just CLI flags.
    93	- Do examine the compaction hook as the phase's highest-priority differentiator for `Code_Environment/Public`.
    94	- Do compare against current Public files so the research reflects today's hybrid retrieval and compaction behavior.
    95	- Do note where the external repo has no extra architecture files or AGENTS file, and treat that absence as evidence about how thin the wrapper really is.
    96	
    97	## 8. Don'ts
    98	
    99	- Do not spend most of the analysis on Mnemosyne's unseen Go internals; they are a dependency, not the plugin repo under study.
   100	- Do not conflate the TypeScript wrapper with the backend search implementation. State clearly when a claim is README-level rather than source-proven.
   101	- Do not ignore the compaction hook; it is the most novel and transferable feature for `Code_Environment/Public`.
   102	- Do not claim Public lacks hybrid BM25+vector memory retrieval when `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts` already proves otherwise.
   103	- Do not try to run the system unless the binary and model are already installed; static analysis is sufficient for this phase, and first-run model download is large.
   104	- Do not collapse this phase into generic FTS5 or BM25 discussion already owned by phases `001` and `003`.
   105	- Do not edit anything under `external/` or outside this phase folder.
   106	
   107	## 9. Examples
   108	
   109	### Example A: Hybrid-search contract finding
   110	
   111	```text
   112	**Finding: Hybrid retrieval is a documented backend contract, not a wrapper-owned algorithm**
   113	- Source: external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/README.md; external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/src/index.ts
   114	- What it does: The README states that Mnemosyne combines SQLite FTS5/BM25 and sqlite-vec cosine similarity with Reciprocal Rank Fusion, while the wrapper simply forwards quoted search queries to `mnemosyne search`.
   115	- Why it matters: Public should not copy README marketing language into implementation plans without deciding whether the value lies in ranking math, wrapper ergonomics, or agent-facing tool guidance.
   116	- Recommendation: prototype later
   117	- Affected area: Spec Kit Memory retrieval positioning, future plugin or wrapper design
   118	- Risk/cost: Medium; backend behavior is not directly inspectable from this repo
   119	```
   120	
   121	### Example B: Compaction-resilience finding
   122	
   123	```text
   124	**Finding: Memory-tool awareness survives compaction through prompt injection**
   125	- Source: external/opencode-mnemosyne-main/src/index.ts; .opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js
   126	- What it does: Mnemosyne appends a compact memory-usage instruction block during `experimental.session.compacting`, while Public's existing compaction plugin injects structural transport context rather than memory-tool reminders.
   127	- Why it matters: Public may already preserve structural state across compaction, but it does not yet remind the agent of concrete memory tools in the same direct way.
   128	- Recommendation: adopt now or prototype later, depending on desired runtime surface
   129	- Affected area: compaction strategy, startup guidance, memory-tool ergonomics
   130	- Risk/cost: Low to medium; prompt noise and duplicate instruction handling must be designed carefully
   131	```
   132	
   133	## 10. Constraints
   134	
   135	### 10.1 Error handling
   136	
   137	- If a claim is only present in the README and not in `src/index.ts`, label it as documented behavior, not source-confirmed implementation.
   138	- If current `Code_Environment/Public` behavior contradicts the phase brief, correct the comparison to match the code.
   139	- If the external repo structure differs from the assumed paths, preserve the same analytical order and document the actual files used.
   140	- If a recommendation depends on backend internals that are not visible here, mark that gap explicitly instead of guessing.
   141	
   142	### 10.2 Scope
   143	
   144	**IN SCOPE**
   145	
   146	- OpenCode plugin entry point and lifecycle
   147	- CLI bridge design and error handling
   148	- five memory tools and their ergonomics
   149	- project/global scoping model
   150	- documented hybrid search contract
   151	- AGENTS.md integration guidance from the README
   152	- compaction-hook behavior and comparison with Public
   153	- offline-first operational tradeoffs
   154	
   155	**OUT OF SCOPE**
   156	
   157	- reverse-engineering the unseen Mnemosyne Go ranking internals
   158	- generic TypeScript style commentary
   159	- plugin publishing workflow details
   160	- broad FTS5 or BM25 primers already covered by other phases
   161	- speculative replacement of Public's existing retrieval stack without evidence
   162	
   163	### 10.3 Prioritization framework
   164	
   165	Rank findings in this order: compaction-resilience leverage, fit with Public's current hybrid retrieval stack, clarity of wrapper-vs-backend boundary, usefulness of project/global scoping ergonomics, offline-first operational value, implementation cost, and clean differentiation from phases `001` and `003`.
   166	
   167	## 11. Deliverables
   168	
   169	- `phase-research-prompt.md` present and tailored specifically to `004-opencode-mnemosyne-main`
   170	- `research/research.md` as the canonical report with at least 5 evidence-backed findings
   171	- explicit comparison against current Public files for hybrid retrieval, session recovery, compaction injection, and CocoIndex positioning
   172	- each finding labeled `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`
   173	- each finding states whether evidence is wrapper-confirmed, README-documented, or both
   174	- `implementation-summary.md` created at the end
   175	- memory saved from this phase folder using `generate-context.js`
   176	
   177	Minimum finding schema:
   178	
   179	- finding title
   180	- exact source evidence
   181	- evidence type: wrapper-confirmed / README-documented / both
   182	- what OpenCode Mnemosyne does
   183	- why it matters for `Code_Environment/Public`
   184	- recommendation: `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`
   185	- affected subsystem
   186	- migration, ambiguity, or validation risk
   187	
   188	## 12. Evaluation Criteria
   189	
   190	- CLEAR target: `>= 40/50`
   191	- RICCE compliance is visible in the prompt:
   192	  - **Role** is specialized in hybrid search, plugin architecture, scoping, and compaction resilience
   193	  - **Instructions** are ordered, concrete, path-specific, and start with the plugin entry point
   194	  - **Context** is domain-specific, cross-phase aware, and corrected to match current Public capabilities
   195	  - **Constraints** clearly separate wrapper evidence from README-level claims
   196	  - **Examples** show strong Mnemosyne-derived findings about hybrid search contracts and compaction hooks
   197	- at least 5 findings are evidence-backed rather than generic
   198	- cross-phase overlap with `001` and `003` is resolved explicitly
   199	- findings compare Mnemosyne with current Public hybrid retrieval and compaction behavior rather than a stale baseline
   200	- project/global scoping, AGENTS guidance, offline-first tradeoffs, and compaction survival are all covered directly
   201	
   202	## 13. Completion Bar
   203	
   204	The phase is only complete when all of the following are true:
   205	
   206	- the reading order started with `src/index.ts`, then `package.json`, then the five tools, then the compaction hook, then `README.md`
   207	- the exact validation command was used for this phase folder
   208	- the exact deep-research topic string appears in the work

   210	- each finding distinguishes wrapper-confirmed behavior from README-documented claims where needed
   211	- `checklist.md` is updated with evidence and completion marks
   212	- `implementation-summary.md` exists
   213	- memory is saved successfully for `004-opencode-mnemosyne-main`
   214	- no edits were made outside the phase folder
   215	- overlap with phases `001` and `003` is explicitly bounded

   217	// resolveRowContextType — now imported from lib/search/search-utils.ts
   218	// resolveEvalScore, collectEvalChannelsFromRow — now imported from lib/telemetry/eval-channel-tracking.ts
   219	
   220	function attachTelemetryMeta(
   221	  response: MCPResponse,
   222	  telemetryPayload: Record<string, unknown>,
   223	): MCPResponse {
   224	  const firstEntry = response?.content?.[0];
   225	  if (!firstEntry || typeof firstEntry.text !== 'string') {
   226	    return response;
   227	  }
   228	
   229	  try {
   230	    const envelope = JSON.parse(firstEntry.text) as Record<string, unknown>;
   231	    const meta = envelope.meta && typeof envelope.meta === 'object'
   232	      ? envelope.meta as Record<string, unknown>
   233	      : {};
   234	    envelope.meta = {
   235	      ...meta,
   236	      _telemetry: telemetryPayload,
   237	    };
   238	
   239	    return {
   240	      ...response,
   241	      content: [{ ...firstEntry, text: JSON.stringify(envelope, null, 2) }],
   242	    };
   243	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   244	    const message = toErrorMessage(error);
   245	    console.warn('[memory-search] Failed to attach telemetry payload:', message);
   246	    return response;
   247	  }
   248	}
   249	
   250	function extractResponseResults(response: MCPResponse): Array<Record<string, unknown>> {
   251	  const firstEntry = response?.content?.[0];
   252	  if (!firstEntry || typeof firstEntry.text !== 'string') {
   253	    return [];
   254	  }
   255	
   256	  try {
   257	    const envelope = JSON.parse(firstEntry.text) as Record<string, unknown>;
   258	    const data = envelope.data && typeof envelope.data === 'object'
   259	      ? envelope.data as Record<string, unknown>
   260	      : null;
   261	    return Array.isArray(data?.results)
   262	      ? data.results as Array<Record<string, unknown>>
   263	      : [];
   264	  } catch {
   265	    return [];
   266	  }
   267	}
   268	
   269	function extractSearchCachePayload(response: MCPResponse): SearchCachePayload | null {
   270	  const firstEntry = response?.content?.[0];
   271	  if (!firstEntry || typeof firstEntry.text !== 'string') {
   272	    return null;
   273	  }
   274	
   275	  try {
   276	    const envelope = JSON.parse(firstEntry.text) as Record<string, unknown>;
   277	    const summary = typeof envelope.summary === 'string' ? envelope.summary : null;
   278	    const data = envelope.data && typeof envelope.data === 'object'
   279	      ? envelope.data as Record<string, unknown>
   280	      : null;
   281	    const hints = Array.isArray(envelope.hints)
   282	      ? envelope.hints.filter((hint): hint is string => typeof hint === 'string')
   283	      : [];
   284	
   285	    if (!summary || !data) {
   286	      return null;
   287	    }
   288	
   289	    return { summary, data, hints };
   290	  } catch {
   291	    return null;
   292	  }
   293	}
   294	
   295	function parseResponseEnvelope(
   296	  response: MCPResponse,
   297	): { firstEntry: { type: 'text'; text: string }; envelope: Record<string, unknown> } | null {
   298	  const firstEntry = response?.content?.[0];
   299	  if (!firstEntry || firstEntry.type !== 'text' || typeof firstEntry.text !== 'string') {
   300	    return null;
   301	  }
   302	
   303	  try {
   304	    return {
   305	      firstEntry,
   306	      envelope: JSON.parse(firstEntry.text) as Record<string, unknown>,
   307	    };
   308	  } catch {
   309	    return null;
   310	  }
   311	}
   312	
   313	function replaceResponseEnvelope(
   314	  response: MCPResponse,
   315	  firstEntry: { type: 'text'; text: string },
   316	  envelope: Record<string, unknown>,
   317	): MCPResponse {
   318	  return {
   319	    ...response,
   320	    content: [{ ...firstEntry, text: JSON.stringify(envelope, null, 2) }],

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-health.ts | sed -n '1,260p'" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Session Health Handler
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	// T018: MCP tool handler for session_health — reports session
     5	// readiness, code graph freshness, and priming status.
     6	
     7	import {
     8	  isSessionPrimed,
     9	  getSessionTimestamps,
    10	  getLastActiveSessionId,
    11	  getCodeGraphStatusSnapshot,
    12	} from '../hooks/memory-surface.js';
    13	
    14	import { computeQualityScore, getLastToolCallAt } from '../lib/session/context-metrics.js';
    15	import { buildStructuralBootstrapContract } from '../lib/session/session-snapshot.js';
    16	import type { StructuralBootstrapContract } from '../lib/session/session-snapshot.js';
    17	import type { QualityScore } from '../lib/session/context-metrics.js';
    18	import {
    19	  createSharedPayloadEnvelope,
    20	  trustStateFromStructuralStatus,
    21	  type SharedPayloadEnvelope,
    22	} from '../lib/context/shared-payload.js';
    23	import {
    24	  buildCodeGraphOpsContract,
    25	  type CodeGraphOpsContract,
    26	} from '../lib/code-graph/ops-hardening.js';
    27	import type { MCPResponse } from '@spec-kit/shared/types';
    28	
    29	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    30	   1. TYPES
    31	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    32	
    33	type SessionStatus = 'ok' | 'warning' | 'stale';
    34	
    35	interface SessionHealthDetails {
    36	  sessionAgeMs: number;
    37	  lastToolCallAgoMs: number;
    38	  graphFreshness: 'fresh' | 'stale' | 'empty' | 'error';
    39	  specFolder: string | null;
    40	  primingStatus: 'primed' | 'not_primed';
    41	}
    42	
    43	interface SessionHealthResult {
    44	  status: SessionStatus;
    45	  details: SessionHealthDetails;
    46	  qualityScore: QualityScore;
    47	  structuralContext?: StructuralBootstrapContract;
    48	  payloadContract?: SharedPayloadEnvelope;
    49	  graphOps?: CodeGraphOpsContract;
    50	  hints: string[];
    51	}
    52	
    53	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    54	   2. CONSTANTS
    55	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    56	
    57	const FIFTEEN_MINUTES_MS = 15 * 60 * 1000;
    58	const SIXTY_MINUTES_MS = 60 * 60 * 1000;
    59	const TWENTY_FOUR_HOURS_MS = 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;
    60	
    61	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    62	   3. HANDLER
    63	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    64	
    65	/** Handle session_health tool call */
    66	export async function handleSessionHealth(): Promise<MCPResponse> {
    67	  const now = Date.now();
    68	  const { serverStartedAt } = getSessionTimestamps();
    69	  // F047: Use context-metrics as single source of truth for lastToolCallAt
    70	  // to eliminate dual-state drift with memory-surface's separate timestamp.
    71	  const metricsLastToolCall = getLastToolCallAt();
    72	  const lastToolCallAt = metricsLastToolCall ?? serverStartedAt;
    73	  const primingSessionId = getLastActiveSessionId();
    74	  const primed = primingSessionId ? isSessionPrimed(primingSessionId) : false;
    75	
    76	  // Determine graph freshness
    77	  let graphFreshness: SessionHealthDetails['graphFreshness'] = 'error';
    78	  // Wire specFolder from context-metrics (fixes P1: dead field)
    79	  let specFolder: string | null = null;
    80	  try {
    81	    const { getSessionMetrics } = await import('../lib/session/context-metrics.js');
    82	    specFolder = getSessionMetrics().currentSpecFolder;
    83	  } catch { /* metrics module may not be loaded */ }
    84	  try {
    85	    const snapshot = getCodeGraphStatusSnapshot();
    86	    if (snapshot.status === 'ok' && snapshot.data) {
    87	      const totalFiles = snapshot.data.totalFiles ?? 0;
    88	      const lastScan = snapshot.data.lastScanAt;
    89	      if (totalFiles === 0) {
    90	        graphFreshness = 'empty';
    91	      } else if (!lastScan || (now - new Date(lastScan).getTime() > TWENTY_FOUR_HOURS_MS)) {
    92	        graphFreshness = 'stale';
    93	      } else {
    94	        graphFreshness = 'fresh';
    95	      }
    96	    }
    97	  } catch {
    98	    graphFreshness = 'error';
    99	  }
   100	
   101	  // Phase 027: Structural bootstrap contract for health surface
   102	  const structuralContext = buildStructuralBootstrapContract('session_health');
   103	
   104	  const sessionAgeMs = now - serverStartedAt;
   105	  const lastToolCallAgoMs = now - lastToolCallAt;
   106	
   107	  // Status determination logic
   108	  let status: SessionStatus;
   109	  if (!primed || lastToolCallAgoMs > SIXTY_MINUTES_MS) {
   110	    status = 'stale';
   111	  } else if (graphFreshness === 'stale' || graphFreshness === 'empty' || lastToolCallAgoMs > FIFTEEN_MINUTES_MS) {
   112	    status = 'warning';
   113	  } else {
   114	    status = 'ok';
   115	  }
   116	
   117	  // Build human-readable hints
   118	  const hints: string[] = [];
   119	  if (!primed) {
   120	    hints.push('Session has not been primed yet. Make any tool call to trigger auto-priming.');
   121	  }
   122	  if (structuralContext.status === 'stale') {
   123	    hints.push('Structural context is stale. Call session_bootstrap to refresh, or run code_graph_scan for a full rescan.');
   124	  } else if (structuralContext.status === 'missing') {
   125	    hints.push('No structural context available. Call session_bootstrap first, then run code_graph_scan.');
   126	  }
   127	  if (lastToolCallAgoMs > SIXTY_MINUTES_MS) {
   128	    hints.push('No tool calls in >60 min. Consider calling `memory_context` to refresh session state.');
   129	  } else if (lastToolCallAgoMs > FIFTEEN_MINUTES_MS) {
   130	    hints.push('No tool calls in >15 min. Session context may be drifting.');
   131	  }
   132	
   133	  // Phase 023: Compute quality score from context metrics
   134	  const qualityScore = computeQualityScore();
   135	
   136	  const payloadContract = createSharedPayloadEnvelope({
   137	    kind: 'health',
   138	    sections: [
   139	      {
   140	        key: 'session-health',
   141	        title: 'Session Health',
   142	        content: `status=${status}; priming=${primed ? 'primed' : 'not_primed'}; graph=${graphFreshness}; specFolder=${specFolder ?? 'none'}`,
   143	        source: 'session',
   144	      },
   145	      {
   146	        key: 'quality-score',
   147	        title: 'Quality Score',
   148	        content: `level=${qualityScore.level}; score=${qualityScore.score}`,
   149	        source: 'operational',
   150	      },
   151	      {
   152	        key: 'structural-context',
   153	        title: 'Structural Context',
   154	        content: structuralContext.summary,
   155	        source: 'code-graph',
   156	      },
   157	    ],
   158	    summary: `Session health is ${status}; graph freshness is ${graphFreshness}; structural status is ${structuralContext.status}`,
   159	    provenance: {
   160	      producer: 'session_health',
   161	      sourceSurface: 'session_health',
   162	      trustState: trustStateFromStructuralStatus(structuralContext.status),
   163	      generatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
   164	      lastUpdated: structuralContext.provenance?.lastUpdated ?? null,
   165	      sourceRefs: ['memory-surface', 'context-metrics', 'session-snapshot'],
   166	    },
   167	  });
   168	  const graphOps = buildCodeGraphOpsContract({
   169	    graphFreshness,
   170	    sourceSurface: 'session_health',
   171	  });
   172	
   173	  const result: SessionHealthResult = {
   174	    status,
   175	    details: {
   176	      sessionAgeMs,
   177	      lastToolCallAgoMs,
   178	      graphFreshness,
   179	      specFolder,
   180	      primingStatus: primed ? 'primed' : 'not_primed',
   181	    },
   182	    qualityScore,
   183	    structuralContext,
   184	    payloadContract,
   185	    graphOps,
   186	    hints,
   187	  };
   188	
   189	  return {
   190	    content: [{
   191	      type: 'text',
   192	      text: JSON.stringify({ status: 'ok', data: result }, null, 2),
   193	    }],
   194	  };
   195	}

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:137:| Codex | `.codex/config.toml` | Checked-in MCP config. Bootstrap parity via `session_bootstrap` MCP tool, not a native SessionStart hook. |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:431:- `memory_stats` (system statistics)
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:436:- `session_health` (session readiness check)
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:437:- `session_bootstrap` (complete session bootstrap)
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:577:### memory_stats: System Statistics
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:579:`memory_stats()` returns counts, dates and top-ranked folders for the memory system. Use it to confirm indexing is working and to inspect database health.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:604:When Z-score analysis signals low-confidence retrieval (insufficient signal in the indexed corpus), the server prepends an evidence gap warning to the LLM payload. This tells the AI assistant that results may be incomplete rather than letting it treat sparse results as authoritative.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/INSTALL_GUIDE.md:614:- `quality`: Result quality score based on embedding confidence and match density

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:249:| Tier 2 | BM25 keyword scoring | FTS5 results below confidence floor |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:285:**Stage 4 -- Filter and annotate**. Enforces score immutability (no score changes after Stage 2). Applies state filtering by minimum state parameter. Annotates results with confidence labels (high/medium/low) and feature flag states.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:311:For low-confidence deep searches, the system has two additional fallback strategies:
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:396:| **REINFORCE** | Similar exists, new one adds value | Both kept, old one gets a confidence boost |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:461:**Result confidence scoring** -- tags each result as high, medium or low confidence using fast heuristics (no LLM needed). Checks: top-K separation, multi-channel agreement, quality score and source document structure.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:521:**Ablation study framework** -- turns off each search channel one at a time and measures quality degradation (Recall@20 delta). Identifies which components are critical.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:545:**Startup/recovery surfaces:** `session_resume`, `session_bootstrap`, and the startup brief now report freshness-aware graph status instead of count-only health. Startup surfaces are intentionally non-mutating snapshots, so later structural reads may still differ if repo state changes.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:601:Resume session with combined memory, code graph and CocoIndex status in a single call. Use when you want the detailed merged resume payload directly. The response carries freshness-aware code-graph status (`fresh`, `stale`, `empty`, `error`) instead of count-only health. For the canonical first-call recovery path on session start or after `/clear`, prefer `session_bootstrap`.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:610:##### `session_bootstrap`
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:612:Complete session bootstrap in one call. This is the canonical first-call recovery step on session start or after `/clear`. It wraps the full `session_resume` payload plus `session_health` and returns context, health, structural readiness and recommended next actions. Startup/bootstrap surfaces are freshness-aware but non-mutating; use `code_graph_scan` when readiness shows an empty or broad full-scan state.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:749:##### `memory_stats`
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:777:##### `session_health`
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:816:##### `memory_validate`
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:818:Tell the system whether a search result was helpful. Helpful results get a confidence boost so they show up more often. Unhelpful results get demoted. Over time, the system learns which memories are genuinely useful, like training a recommendation engine with thumbs-up and thumbs-down.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:823:| `wasUseful` | boolean | **Required.** Was this memory helpful? |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:1030:##### `eval_run_ablation`
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:1046:##### `eval_reporting_dashboard`
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:1535:| Resume a session from scratch | `session_bootstrap` | Use as the first recovery call on session start or after `/clear` |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:1541:| See what is indexed | `memory_list` + `memory_stats` | Browse and count |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:1607:**What you see**: `memory_stats` shows fewer memories than expected.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:1699:A typical project with a few hundred memory files uses 10-50 MB. The vector table (1024-dimension float32 embeddings) is the largest contributor. Check with `memory_stats` using `includeScores: true`.
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/README.md:1729:Set the flag to `false` or `0` in your environment, restart the server and the pipeline falls back to the last working configuration. The full procedure is in `../references/workflows/rollback_runbook.md`. Use `eval_reporting_dashboard` to verify metrics returned to baseline.

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/scoring/README.md:48:| **HVR Integration** | Human Validation Rate integration for confidence-weighted scoring (Spec 137) |
.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/scoring/README.md:125:HVR integration extends the confidence tracking system by incorporating human validation signals into composite scoring. The HVR score reflects how often users confirm a memory as useful versus not useful, creating a feedback-weighted confidence metric.

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:119:**Score immutability invariant**: Stage 4 MUST NOT modify scores. Enforced via compile-time `Stage4ReadonlyRow` readonly fields and runtime `captureScoreSnapshot` / `verifyScoreInvariant` defence-in-depth. Applies memory-state filtering (HOT/WARM/COLD/DORMANT/ARCHIVED with per-tier limits), evidence gap detection (Z-score confidence check), quality floor (`QUALITY_FLOOR=0.005`), and token budget truncation.

.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:751:`wasUseful=false` validations apply a demotion multiplier to composite scores. Penalty: -0.1 per negative validation, floor at 0.3 (never suppress below 30%). 30-day half-life recovery: penalty halves over time if no further negative feedback. Gated via `SPECKIT_NEGATIVE_FEEDBACK` (default ON).
