 succeeded in 0ms:
    96	/**
    97	 * Record a validation event for a memory and persist confidence counters.
    98	 *
    99	 * Stage 2 integration hooks:
   100	 * - This function updates `memory_index.confidence` and `validation_count`,
   101	 *   establishing durable feedback state for search-stage signal consumers.
   102	 * - For `wasUseful=false`, `handlers/checkpoints.ts` pairs this update with
   103	 *   `recordNegativeFeedbackEvent`, and Stage 2 (`search/pipeline/stage2-fusion.ts`)
   104	 *   reads those events via `getNegativeFeedbackStats` to apply demotion.
   105	 * - Positive validations can trigger auto-promotion (`importance_tier` changes),
   106	 *   which Stage 2 validation metadata scoring treats as a quality signal.
   107	 */
   108	export function recordValidation(db: Database, memoryId: number, wasUseful: boolean): ValidationResult {
   109	  try {
   110	    // Keep read/compute/write in one transaction so concurrent updates cannot drop validations.
   111	    return db.transaction(() => {
   112	      // Read first so this update derives from the latest persisted values.
   113	      const memory = db.prepare(`
   114	        SELECT confidence, validation_count, importance_tier FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?
   115	      `).get(memoryId) as MemoryConfidenceRow | undefined;
   116	
   117	      if (!memory) {
   118	        throw new Error(`Memory not found: ${memoryId}`);
   119	      }
   120	
   121	      const currentConfidence = memory.confidence ?? CONFIDENCE_BASE;
   122	      let newConfidence: number;
   123	
   124	      if (wasUseful) {
   125	        newConfidence = Math.min(currentConfidence + CONFIDENCE_POSITIVE_INCREMENT, CONFIDENCE_MAX);
   126	      } else {
   127	        newConfidence = Math.max(currentConfidence - CONFIDENCE_NEGATIVE_DECREMENT, CONFIDENCE_MIN);
   128	      }
   129	
   130	      const currentValidationCount = memory.validationCount ?? memory.validation_count ?? 0;
   131	      const newValidationCount = currentValidationCount + 1;
   132	
   133	      db.prepare(`
   134	        UPDATE memory_index
   135	        SET confidence = ?, validation_count = ?, updated_at = ?
   136	        WHERE id = ?
   137	      `).run(newConfidence, newValidationCount, new Date().toISOString(), memoryId);
   138	
   139	      const priorNegativeValidationCount = getNegativeValidationCount(db, memoryId);
   140	      const effectiveNegativeValidationCount = wasUseful
   141	        ? priorNegativeValidationCount
   142	        : priorNegativeValidationCount + 1;
   143	      const positiveValidationCount = resolvePositiveValidationCount(
   144	        newValidationCount,
   145	        effectiveNegativeValidationCount
   146	      );
   147	
   148	      // Report eligibility only; promotion is intentionally explicit and separate.
   149	      const promotionEligible = isPromotionEligible(
   150	        memory.importance_tier,
   151	        newConfidence,
   152	        positiveValidationCount
   153	      );
   154	
   155	      if (!wasUseful) {
   156	        const chunkId = memoryId;
   157	        console.warn('[confidence-tracker] negative feedback recorded', {
   158	          chunkId,
   159	          previousConfidence: currentConfidence,
   160	          newConfidence,
   161	          decrement: CONFIDENCE_NEGATIVE_DECREMENT,
   162	        });
   163	      }
   164	
   165	      return {
   166	        confidence: newConfidence,
   167	        validationCount: newValidationCount,
   168	        positiveValidationCount,
   169	        promotionEligible,
   170	        wasPromoted: false,
   171	      };
   172	    })();
   173	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   174	    // T-07: Replace success-shaped fallback with explicit failure signaling.
   175	    // Previously returned default values that looked like success, hiding DB errors
   176	    // From callers and allowing downstream side-effects to proceed on stale data.
   177	    console.error(`[confidence-tracker] recordValidation failed for memory ${memoryId}:`, error);
   178	    throw error;
   179	  }
   180	}
   181	
   182	/**
   183	 * Get current confidence score for a memory.
   184	 */
   185	export function getConfidenceScore(db: Database, memoryId: number): number {
   186	  try {
   187	    // DB op 3: SELECT
   188	    const memory = db.prepare(`
   189	      SELECT confidence FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?
   190	    `).get(memoryId) as { confidence?: number } | undefined;
   191	
   192	    if (!memory) {
   193	      throw new Error(`Memory not found: ${memoryId}`);
   194	    }
   195	
   196	    return memory.confidence ?? CONFIDENCE_BASE;
   197	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   198	    console.error(`[confidence-tracker] getConfidenceScore failed for memory ${memoryId}:`, error);
   199	    return CONFIDENCE_BASE;
   200	  }
   201	}
   202	
   203	/**
   204	 * Check if a memory is eligible for promotion to critical tier.
   205	 */
   206	export function checkPromotionEligible(db: Database, memoryId: number): boolean {
   207	  try {
   208	    const memory = db.prepare(`
   209	      SELECT confidence, validation_count, importance_tier FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?
   210	    `).get(memoryId) as MemoryConfidenceRow | undefined;
   211	
   212	    if (!memory) {
   213	      return false;
   214	    }
   215	
   216	    // Top-tier memories should not be re-promoted.
   217	    if (memory.importance_tier === 'critical' || memory.importance_tier === 'constitutional') {
   218	      return false;
   219	    }
   220	
   221	    const confidence = memory.confidence ?? CONFIDENCE_BASE;
   222	    const validationCount = memory.validationCount ?? memory.validation_count ?? 0;
   223	    const negativeValidationCount = getNegativeValidationCount(db, memoryId);
   224	    const positiveValidationCount = resolvePositiveValidationCount(validationCount, negativeValidationCount);
   225	
   226	    return isPromotionEligible(memory.importance_tier, confidence, positiveValidationCount);
   227	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   228	    console.error(`[confidence-tracker] checkPromotionEligible failed for memory ${memoryId}:`, error);
   229	    return false;
   230	  }
   231	}
   232	
   233	/**
   234	 * Promote a memory to critical tier.
   235	 */
   236	export function promoteToCritical(db: Database, memoryId: number): boolean {
   237	  try {
   238	    if (!checkPromotionEligible(db, memoryId)) {
   239	      const memory = db.prepare(`
   240	        SELECT confidence, validation_count, importance_tier FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?
   241	      `).get(memoryId) as MemoryConfidenceRow | undefined;
   242	
   243	      if (!memory) {
   244	        throw new Error(`Memory not found: ${memoryId}`);
   245	      }
   246	
   247	      if (memory.importance_tier === 'critical' || memory.importance_tier === 'constitutional') {
   248	        return false;
   249	      }
   250	
   251	      const validationCount = memory.validationCount ?? memory.validation_count ?? 0;
   252	      const negativeValidationCount = getNegativeValidationCount(db, memoryId);
   253	      const positiveValidationCount = resolvePositiveValidationCount(validationCount, negativeValidationCount);
   254	      throw new Error(
   255	        `Memory ${memoryId} not eligible for promotion. ` +
   256	        `Requires confidence >= ${PROMOTION_CONFIDENCE_THRESHOLD} (current: ${memory.confidence ?? CONFIDENCE_BASE}) ` +
   257	        `and positive_validation_count >= ${PROMOTION_VALIDATION_THRESHOLD} (current: ${positiveValidationCount})`
   258	      );
   259	    }
   260	
   261	    // Keep promotion explicit so tier changes always leave an auditable event.
   262	    db.prepare(`
   263	      UPDATE memory_index
   264	      SET importance_tier = 'critical', updated_at = ?
   265	      WHERE id = ?
   266	    `).run(new Date().toISOString(), memoryId);
   267	
   268	    console.warn(`[confidence-tracker] Memory ${memoryId} promoted to critical tier`);
   269	
   270	    return true;
   271	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   272	    console.error(`[confidence-tracker] promoteToCritical failed for memory ${memoryId}:`, error);
   273	    return false;
   274	  }
   275	}

   278	 * Get full confidence info for a memory.
   279	 *
   280	 * Stage 2 integration hook: handlers can surface this snapshot in telemetry

   272	
   273	function recordQualityProxy(
   274	  t: RetrievalTelemetry,
   275	  results: Array<{ score?: number; similarity?: number }>,
   276	  boostDelta: number,
   277	  extractionCount: number,
   278	): void {
   279	  if (!t.enabled) return;
   280	
   281	  const count = Array.isArray(results) ? results.length : 0;
   282	  t.quality.resultCount = count;
   283	  t.quality.extractionCountInSession = Math.max(0, extractionCount || 0);
   284	
   285	  if (count > 0) {
   286	    const scores = results.map((r) => {
   287	      if (typeof r.score === 'number' && Number.isFinite(r.score)) return r.score;
   288	      if (typeof r.similarity === 'number' && Number.isFinite(r.similarity)) return r.similarity / 100;
   289	      return 0;
   290	    });
   291	    // Reduce avoids stack overflow on arrays >100K elements (spread pushes all onto call stack)
   292	    t.quality.topResultScore = scores.reduce((a, b) => Math.max(a, b), -Infinity);
   293	    t.quality.avgRelevanceScore = scores.reduce((sum, s) => sum + s, 0) / count;
   294	  }
   295	
   296	  t.quality.boostImpactDelta = Number.isFinite(boostDelta) ? boostDelta : 0;
   297	  t.quality.qualityProxyScore = computeQualityProxy(t);
   298	}
   299	
   300	function recordTracePayload(t: RetrievalTelemetry, payload: unknown): boolean {
   301	  if (!t.enabled) return false;
   302	
   303	  const sanitized = sanitizeRetrievalTracePayload(payload);
   304	  if (!sanitized) {
   305	    delete t.tracePayload;
   306	    return false;
   307	  }
   308	
   309	  t.tracePayload = sanitized;
   310	  return true;
   311	}
   312	
   313	function recordTransitionDiagnostics(
   314	  t: RetrievalTelemetry,
   315	  transition?: SessionTransitionTrace,
   316	): void {
   317	  if (!t.enabled) return;
   318	  if (!transition) {
   319	    delete t.transitionDiagnostics;
   320	    return;
   321	  }
   322	
   323	  t.transitionDiagnostics = {
   324	    previousState: transition.previousState,
   325	    currentState: transition.currentState,
   326	    confidence: Math.max(0, Math.min(1, transition.confidence)),
   327	    signalSources: Array.isArray(transition.signalSources)
   328	      ? transition.signalSources.filter((signal): signal is SessionTransitionTrace['signalSources'][number] => typeof signal === 'string')
   329	      : [],
   330	    reason: typeof transition.reason === 'string' && transition.reason.length > 0 ? transition.reason : null,
   331	  };
   332	}
   333	
   334	function recordGraphWalkDiagnostics(
   335	  t: RetrievalTelemetry,
   336	  update: Partial<GraphWalkDiagnostics>,
   337	): void {
   338	  if (!t.enabled) return;
   339	  const current: GraphWalkDiagnostics = t.graphWalkDiagnostics ?? {
   340	    rolloutState: 'off',
   341	    rowsWithGraphContribution: 0,
   342	    rowsWithAppliedBonus: 0,
   343	    capAppliedCount: 0,
   344	    maxRaw: 0,
   345	    maxNormalized: 0,
   346	    maxAppliedBonus: 0,
   347	  };
   348	
   349	  t.graphWalkDiagnostics = {
   350	    rolloutState: update.rolloutState ?? current.rolloutState,
   351	    rowsWithGraphContribution: typeof update.rowsWithGraphContribution === 'number' && Number.isFinite(update.rowsWithGraphContribution)
   352	      ? Math.max(0, Math.floor(update.rowsWithGraphContribution))
   353	      : current.rowsWithGraphContribution,
   354	    rowsWithAppliedBonus: typeof update.rowsWithAppliedBonus === 'number' && Number.isFinite(update.rowsWithAppliedBonus)
   355	      ? Math.max(0, Math.floor(update.rowsWithAppliedBonus))
   356	      : current.rowsWithAppliedBonus,
   357	    capAppliedCount: typeof update.capAppliedCount === 'number' && Number.isFinite(update.capAppliedCount)
   358	      ? Math.max(0, Math.floor(update.capAppliedCount))
   359	      : current.capAppliedCount,
   360	    maxRaw: typeof update.maxRaw === 'number' && Number.isFinite(update.maxRaw)
   361	      ? Math.max(0, update.maxRaw)
   362	      : current.maxRaw,
   363	    maxNormalized: typeof update.maxNormalized === 'number' && Number.isFinite(update.maxNormalized)
   364	      ? Math.max(0, update.maxNormalized)
   365	      : current.maxNormalized,
   366	    maxAppliedBonus: typeof update.maxAppliedBonus === 'number' && Number.isFinite(update.maxAppliedBonus)
   367	      ? Math.max(0, update.maxAppliedBonus)
   368	      : current.maxAppliedBonus,
   369	  };
   370	}
   371	
   372	function recordLifecycleForecastDiagnostics(
   373	  t: RetrievalTelemetry,
   374	  forecast: Record<string, unknown> | null | undefined,
   375	  context: {
   376	    state?: string | null;
   377	    progress?: number;
   378	    filesProcessed?: number;
   379	    filesTotal?: number;
   380	  } = {},

   386	    : {};

   388	    ? candidate.riskSignals.filter((signal): signal is string => typeof signal === 'string')
   389	    : [];
   390	
   391	  t.lifecycleForecastDiagnostics = {
   392	    state: typeof context.state === 'string' && context.state.length > 0 ? context.state : null,
   393	    progress:
   394	      typeof context.progress === 'number' && Number.isFinite(context.progress)
   395	        ? Math.max(0, Math.min(100, context.progress))
   396	        : 0,
   397	    filesProcessed:
   398	      typeof context.filesProcessed === 'number' && Number.isFinite(context.filesProcessed)
   399	        ? Math.max(0, Math.trunc(context.filesProcessed))
   400	        : 0,
   401	    filesTotal:
   402	      typeof context.filesTotal === 'number' && Number.isFinite(context.filesTotal)
   403	        ? Math.max(0, Math.trunc(context.filesTotal))
   404	        : 0,
   405	    etaSeconds:
   406	      typeof candidate.etaSeconds === 'number' && Number.isFinite(candidate.etaSeconds)
   407	        ? Math.max(0, candidate.etaSeconds)
   408	        : null,
   409	    etaConfidence:
   410	      typeof candidate.etaConfidence === 'number' && Number.isFinite(candidate.etaConfidence)
   411	        ? Math.max(0, Math.min(1, candidate.etaConfidence))
   412	        : null,
   413	    failureRisk:
   414	      typeof candidate.failureRisk === 'number' && Number.isFinite(candidate.failureRisk)
   415	        ? Math.max(0, Math.min(1, candidate.failureRisk))
   416	        : null,
   417	    riskSignals,
   418	    ...(typeof candidate.caveat === 'string' && candidate.caveat.length > 0
   419	      ? { caveat: candidate.caveat }
   420	      : {}),

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Eval Quality Proxy (T006G)
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	// Feature catalog: Quality proxy formula
     5	// Automated quality proxy metric that correlates with manual
     6	// Quality assessment. Pure function, no DB access, no side effects.
     7	//
     8	// Formula:
     9	// QualityProxy = avgRelevance * 0.40 + topResult * 0.25
    10	// + countSaturation * 0.20 + latencyPenalty * 0.15
    11	//
    12	// All components normalised to [0, 1]. Output range: [0, 1].
    13	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    14	   1. CONSTANTS
    15	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    16	
    17	/** Default latency target in milliseconds. */
    18	const DEFAULT_LATENCY_TARGET_MS = 500;
    19	
    20	/** Weights for each component — must sum to 1.0. */
    21	const WEIGHTS = {
    22	  avgRelevance: 0.4,
    23	  topResult: 0.25,
    24	  countSaturation: 0.2,
    25	  latencyPenalty: 0.15,
    26	} as const;
    27	
    28	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    29	   2. TYPES
    30	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    31	
    32	/** Input parameters for the quality proxy computation. */
    33	export interface QualityProxyInput {
    34	  /**
    35	   * Average relevance score of returned results.
    36	   * Should be normalised to [0, 1] — typically avgRelevance / 3
    37	   * when raw relevance is on a 0-3 scale.
    38	   */
    39	  avgRelevance: number;
    40	
    41	  /**
    42	   * Relevance of the top result (rank 1).
    43	   * Should be normalised to [0, 1] — typically relevance / 3
    44	   * when raw relevance is on a 0-3 scale.
    45	   */
    46	  topResultRelevance: number;
    47	
    48	  /** Number of results actually returned by the search. */
    49	  resultCount: number;
    50	
    51	  /**
    52	   * Expected / desired number of results (e.g. the limit parameter
    53	   * passed to the search call).
    54	   * Must be > 0 to avoid division by zero; defaults to 1 when ≤ 0.
    55	   */
    56	  expectedCount: number;
    57	
    58	  /** Actual search latency in milliseconds. */
    59	  latencyMs: number;
    60	
    61	  /**
    62	   * Target latency in milliseconds.
    63	   * Defaults to 500 ms when omitted or ≤ 0.
    64	   */
    65	  latencyTargetMs?: number;
    66	}
    67	
    68	/** Breakdown of each component's weighted contribution. */
    69	export interface QualityProxyComponents {
    70	  /** avgRelevance × 0.40 */
    71	  avgRelevance: number;
    72	  /** topResultRelevance × 0.25 */
    73	  topResult: number;
    74	  /** countSaturation × 0.20 */
    75	  countSaturation: number;
    76	  /** latencyScore × 0.15 */
    77	  latencyPenalty: number;
    78	}
    79	
    80	/** Result of the quality proxy computation. */
    81	export interface QualityProxyResult {
    82	  /** Composite quality score in [0, 1]. */
    83	  score: number;
    84	  /** Weighted contribution of each individual component. */
    85	  components: QualityProxyComponents;
    86	  /** Human-readable quality tier: 'excellent' | 'good' | 'acceptable' | 'poor'. */
    87	  interpretation: 'excellent' | 'good' | 'acceptable' | 'poor';
    88	}
    89	
    90	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    91	   3. INTERNAL HELPERS
    92	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
    93	
    94	/**
    95	 * Clamp a value to [min, max].
    96	 */
    97	function clamp(value: number, min: number, max: number): number {
    98	  return Math.min(max, Math.max(min, value));
    99	}
   100	
   101	/**
   102	 * Compute the count saturation component.
   103	 * Returns min(1, resultCount / expectedCount).
   104	 * When expectedCount ≤ 0 it is treated as 1 to avoid division by zero.
   105	 */
   106	function computeCountSaturation(
   107	  resultCount: number,
   108	  expectedCount: number,
   109	): number {
   110	  const safeExpected = expectedCount > 0 ? expectedCount : 1;
   111	  return Math.min(1, resultCount / safeExpected);
   112	}
   113	
   114	/**
   115	 * Compute the latency penalty component.
   116	 * Returns max(0, 1 − latencyMs / latencyTargetMs).
   117	 *   - 1.0 at latencyMs = 0 (perfect — full credit)
   118	 *   - 0.0 at latencyMs ≥ latencyTargetMs (at or over target)
   119	 * When latencyTargetMs ≤ 0 it is treated as DEFAULT_LATENCY_TARGET_MS.
   120	 */
   121	function computeLatencyScore(
   122	  latencyMs: number,
   123	  latencyTargetMs: number,
   124	): number {
   125	  const safeTarget =
   126	    latencyTargetMs > 0 ? latencyTargetMs : DEFAULT_LATENCY_TARGET_MS;
   127	  return Math.max(0, 1 - latencyMs / safeTarget);
   128	}
   129	
   130	/**
   131	 * Map a composite score to an interpretation label.
   132	 *   ≥ 0.8  → 'excellent'
   133	 *   ≥ 0.6  → 'good'
   134	 *   ≥ 0.4  → 'acceptable'
   135	 *   <  0.4 → 'poor'
   136	 */
   137	function interpretScore(
   138	  score: number,
   139	): 'excellent' | 'good' | 'acceptable' | 'poor' {
   140	  if (score >= 0.8) return 'excellent';
   141	  if (score >= 0.6) return 'good';
   142	  if (score >= 0.4) return 'acceptable';
   143	  return 'poor';
   144	}
   145	
   146	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   147	   4. CORE FUNCTION
   148	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   149	
   150	/**
   151	 * Compute the quality proxy score for a single search result set.
   152	 *
   153	 * All four components are normalised to [0, 1] before weighting:
   154	 *
   155	 *   avgRelevance    — direct pass-through (caller normalises to [0,1])
   156	 *   topResult       — direct pass-through (caller normalises to [0,1])
   157	 *   countSaturation — min(1, resultCount / expectedCount)
   158	 *   latencyPenalty  — max(0, 1 − latencyMs / latencyTargetMs)
   159	 *
   160	 * The final score is clamped to [0, 1] to guard against floating-point
   161	 * rounding errors.
   162	 *
   163	 * @returns QualityProxyResult with composite score, per-component
   164	 *          weighted contributions, and a human-readable interpretation.
   165	 */
   166	export function computeQualityProxy(
   167	  input: QualityProxyInput,
   168	): QualityProxyResult {
   169	  const {
   170	    avgRelevance,
   171	    topResultRelevance,
   172	    resultCount,
   173	    expectedCount,
   174	    latencyMs,
   175	    latencyTargetMs = DEFAULT_LATENCY_TARGET_MS,
   176	  } = input;
   177	
   178	  // Clamp raw inputs to [0, 1] — guard against out-of-range values
   179	  const clampedAvgRelevance = clamp(avgRelevance, 0, 1);
   180	  const clampedTopResult = clamp(topResultRelevance, 0, 1);
   181	
   182	  // Compute raw (unweighted) component values in [0, 1]
   183	  const rawCountSaturation = computeCountSaturation(resultCount, expectedCount);
   184	  const rawLatencyScore = computeLatencyScore(latencyMs, latencyTargetMs);
   185	
   186	  // Apply weights to produce each component's contribution
   187	  const components: QualityProxyComponents = {
   188	    avgRelevance: clampedAvgRelevance * WEIGHTS.avgRelevance,
   189	    topResult: clampedTopResult * WEIGHTS.topResult,
   190	    countSaturation: rawCountSaturation * WEIGHTS.countSaturation,
   191	    latencyPenalty: rawLatencyScore * WEIGHTS.latencyPenalty,
   192	  };
   193	
   194	  // Sum all weighted components and clamp to [0, 1]
   195	  const rawScore =
   196	    components.avgRelevance +
   197	    components.topResult +
   198	    components.countSaturation +
   199	    components.latencyPenalty;
   200	
   201	  const score = clamp(rawScore, 0, 1);
   202	
   203	  return {
   204	    score,
   205	    components,
   206	    interpretation: interpretScore(score),
   207	  };
   208	}
   209	
   210	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   211	   5. EXPORTS (re-export constants for test convenience)
   212	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
   213	
   214	export { WEIGHTS, DEFAULT_LATENCY_TARGET_MS };

   601	
   602	async function resolveDispatchGraphContext(
   603	  toolName: string,
   604	  args: Record<string, unknown>,
   605	): Promise<DispatchGraphContextMeta | null> {
   606	  if (GRAPH_CONTEXT_EXCLUDED_TOOLS.has(toolName)) {
   607	    return null;
   608	  }
   609	
   610	  const filePaths = extractFilePathsFromToolArgs(args);
   611	  if (filePaths.length === 0) {
   612	    return null;
   613	  }
   614	
   615	  const startedAt = Date.now();
   616	  let timeoutHandle: ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | undefined;
   617	
   618	  const buildPromise = new Promise<DispatchGraphContextMeta>((resolve) => {
   619	    queueMicrotask(() => {
   620	      try {
   621	        const context = buildDispatchGraphContext(
   622	          filePaths,
   623	          startedAt + GRAPH_ENRICHMENT_TIMEOUT_MS,
   624	        );
   625	
   626	        if (Date.now() - startedAt >= GRAPH_ENRICHMENT_TIMEOUT_MS) {
   627	          return;
   628	        }
   629	
   630	        resolve({
   631	          ...context,
   632	          latencyMs: Date.now() - startedAt,
   633	        });
   634	      } catch (error: unknown) {
   635	        resolve({
   636	          status: 'unavailable',
   637	          source: 'tool-dispatch',
   638	          fileCount: filePaths.length,
   639	          filePaths,
   640	          latencyMs: Date.now() - startedAt,
   641	          error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),
   642	        });
   643	      }
   644	    });
   645	  });
   646	
   647	  const timeoutPromise = new Promise<DispatchGraphContextMeta>((resolve) => {
   648	    timeoutHandle = setTimeout(() => {
   649	      resolve({
   650	        status: 'timeout',
   651	        source: 'tool-dispatch',
   652	        fileCount: filePaths.length,
   653	        filePaths,
   654	        latencyMs: Date.now() - startedAt,
   655	      });
   656	    }, GRAPH_ENRICHMENT_TIMEOUT_MS);
   657	  });
   658	
   659	  const graphContext = await Promise.race([buildPromise, timeoutPromise]);
   660	  if (timeoutHandle) {
   661	    clearTimeout(timeoutHandle);
   662	  }
   663	  return graphContext;
   664	}
   665	
   666	function injectSessionPrimeHints(
   667	  envelope: Record<string, unknown>,
   668	  meta: Record<string, unknown>,
   669	  sessionPrimeContext: AutoSurfaceResult,
   670	): void {
   671	  const hints = Array.isArray(envelope.hints)
   672	    ? envelope.hints.filter((hint): hint is string => typeof hint === 'string')
   673	    : [];
   674	  envelope.hints = hints;
   675	
   676	  const constitutionalCount = Array.isArray(sessionPrimeContext.constitutional)
   677	    ? sessionPrimeContext.constitutional.length
   678	    : 0;
   679	  const codeGraphStatus = sessionPrimeContext.codeGraphStatus;
   680	  const codeGraphState = codeGraphStatus?.status === 'ok'
   681	    ? 'loaded code graph status'
   682	    : 'code graph status unavailable';
   683	
   684	  hints.push(
   685	    `Session priming: loaded ${constitutionalCount} constitutional memories and ${codeGraphState}`
   686	  );
   687	
   688	  // T018: Include Prime Package hints for non-hook CLIs
   689	  const pkg = sessionPrimeContext.primePackage;
   690	  if (pkg) {
   691	    if (pkg.specFolder) {
   692	      hints.push(`Active spec folder: ${pkg.specFolder}`);
   693	    }
   694	    hints.push(`Code graph: ${pkg.codeGraphStatus}, CocoIndex: ${pkg.cocoIndexAvailable ? 'available' : 'not installed'}`);
   695	    if (pkg.recommendedCalls.length > 0) {
   696	      hints.push(`Recommended next calls: ${pkg.recommendedCalls.join(', ')}`);
   697	    }
   698	  }
   699	
   700	  meta.sessionPriming = sessionPrimeContext;
   701	}
   702	
   703	async function getMemoryStats(): Promise<DynamicMemoryStats> {
   704	  try {
   705	    const response = await handleMemoryStats({
   706	      folderRanking: 'count',
   707	      includeArchived: true,
   708	      limit: 100,
   709	    });
   710	    const payload = response?.content?.[0]?.text;
   711	    if (typeof payload !== 'string' || payload.length === 0) {
   712	      return { totalMemories: 0, specFolderCount: 0, activeCount: 0, staleCount: 0 };

   714	
   715	    const parsed = JSON.parse(payload) as Record<string, unknown>;

   717	    const byStatus = (data.byStatus ?? {}) as Record<string, unknown>;
   718	    const topFolders = Array.isArray(data.topFolders) ? data.topFolders : [];
   719	    const success = typeof byStatus.success === 'number' ? byStatus.success : 0;
   720	    const pending = typeof byStatus.pending === 'number' ? byStatus.pending : 0;

# Iteration 028: BENCHMARK & METRICS

## Focus
BENCHMARK & METRICS: What does this system measure? What metrics should we track for memory quality, retrieval accuracy, and system health?

## Findings
### Finding 1: Mnemosyne’s wrapper is operationally observable, but benchmark-blind
- **Source**: [index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts), [package.json](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/package.json), [README.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/README.md)
- **What it does**: The wrapper logs command execution, exit failures, and output size, and turns empty recall output into `"No memories found."` / `"No global memories found."` It does not expose retrieval latency, ranking confidence, recall, precision, health status, or any benchmark harness. The README documents hybrid search and first-run model download, but those are backend claims, not measured plugin metrics.
- **Why it matters**: Mnemosyne is useful as a plugin-ergonomics reference, but not as a benchmark source of truth. We should not treat its README as evidence that retrieval quality is good or stable.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 2: Public already has first-class session and recovery health scoring
- **Source**: [context-metrics.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/session/context-metrics.ts), [session-health.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-health.ts), [session-bootstrap.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts), [context-server.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts)
- **What it does**: Public computes a session quality score from recency, memory recovery, graph freshness, and continuity; `session_health` surfaces `ok` / `warning` / `stale`; `session_bootstrap` records bootstrap duration and completeness; startup logs report `memory_index` and `active_memory_projection` counts.
- **Why it matters**: Our system-health benchmark should center on session readiness and recovery quality, not just database uptime.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 3: Public already has a real retrieval benchmark lane
- **Source**: [eval-reporting.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/eval-reporting.ts), [retrieval-telemetry.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/telemetry/retrieval-telemetry.ts), [eval-quality-proxy.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/eval/eval-quality-proxy.ts), [memory-search.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts)
- **What it does**: `eval_run_ablation` measures channel ablations with `Recall@K` and K-sensitivity; retrieval telemetry tracks candidate/fusion/rerank/boost latency, fallback, mode selection, result count, average relevance, top-result score, boost delta, and a composite quality proxy.
- **Why it matters**: For retrieval accuracy, the canonical metrics are already here: `Recall@K`, ablation delta by channel, K-sensitivity, quality proxy, fallback rate, and latency breakdown.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 4: Public’s memory quality model is feedback-based and durable
- **Source**: [checkpoints.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/checkpoints.ts), [confidence-tracker.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/scoring/confidence-tracker.ts), [vector-index-queries.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-queries.ts)
- **What it does**: `memory_validate` updates `confidence`, `validationCount`, `positiveValidationCount`, promotion eligibility, negative-feedback events, and learned feedback. The index also exposes `access_count`, `last_accessed`, and cleanup candidates driven by low confidence and low use.
- **Why it matters**: Memory quality should be tracked with usefulness confirmations and longitudinal retention behavior, not only retrieval scores.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 5: Public already measures index integrity and storage health
- **Source**: [memory-crud-health.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts), [memory-crud-stats.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-stats.ts), [context-server.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts)
- **What it does**: Public reports embedding readiness and retry stats, vector availability, FTS5 parity, alias conflicts, orphan-edge cleanup, by-status embedding counts, trigger totals, tier breakdown, database size, last indexed time, and startup projection health.
- **Why it matters**: System health must include integrity metrics: embedding backlog, FTS drift, alias divergence, graph orphan rate, storage growth, and projection consistency.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 6: The real missing benchmark is compaction continuity
- **Source**: [index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts), [spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js), [session-bootstrap.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts)
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne injects memory-tool guidance during `experimental.session.compacting`. Public injects structural transport blocks and records bootstrap duration/completeness, but neither system turns post-compaction recovery into an explicit benchmark.
- **Why it matters**: For long-running agent memory systems, the critical failure mode is often “after compaction, can the agent recover fast and correctly?”
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: high

## Assessment
- New information ratio: 0.68
- The external system mostly measures execution success, not memory quality or retrieval accuracy. Public already has the stronger measurement stack.
- The metrics we should track are:
- Memory quality: `confidence`, `validationCount`, `positiveValidationCount`, negative-feedback rate, `access_count`, `last_accessed`, cleanup-candidate rate, archive/unarchive rate.
- Retrieval accuracy: `Recall@K`, channel ablation delta, K-sensitivity, average result confidence, evidence-gap rate, `no_results` / `low_confidence` / `partial` distribution, stage latency breakdown.
- System health: bootstrap duration/completeness, session quality score, graph freshness, embedding status counts, FTS parity drift, alias conflicts, retry stats, database size, startup projection consistency.
- Validation: `bash .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/validate.sh "/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main" --strict` returned `RESULT: PASSED` with `Errors: 0  Warnings: 0`, plus the known read-only warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`.

## Recommended Next Focus
Define a compaction-continuity benchmark for Public: measure recovery success after `experimental.session.compacting`, time to first successful `memory_context` / `memory_search`, and whether scoped/private/shared retrieval remains correct after bootstrap and compaction.

### Finding 3: Public already has a real retrieval benchmark lane
- **Source**: [eval-reporting.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/eval-reporting.ts), [retrieval-telemetry.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/telemetry/retrieval-telemetry.ts), [eval-quality-proxy.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/eval/eval-quality-proxy.ts), [memory-search.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts)
- **What it does**: `eval_run_ablation` measures channel ablations with `Recall@K` and K-sensitivity; retrieval telemetry tracks candidate/fusion/rerank/boost latency, fallback, mode selection, result count, average relevance, top-result score, boost delta, and a composite quality proxy.
- **Why it matters**: For retrieval accuracy, the canonical metrics are already here: `Recall@K`, ablation delta by channel, K-sensitivity, quality proxy, fallback rate, and latency breakdown.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 4: Public’s memory quality model is feedback-based and durable
- **Source**: [checkpoints.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/checkpoints.ts), [confidence-tracker.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/scoring/confidence-tracker.ts), [vector-index-queries.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-queries.ts)
- **What it does**: `memory_validate` updates `confidence`, `validationCount`, `positiveValidationCount`, promotion eligibility, negative-feedback events, and learned feedback. The index also exposes `access_count`, `last_accessed`, and cleanup candidates driven by low confidence and low use.
- **Why it matters**: Memory quality should be tracked with usefulness confirmations and longitudinal retention behavior, not only retrieval scores.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 5: Public already measures index integrity and storage health
- **Source**: [memory-crud-health.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts), [memory-crud-stats.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-stats.ts), [context-server.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts)
- **What it does**: Public reports embedding readiness and retry stats, vector availability, FTS5 parity, alias conflicts, orphan-edge cleanup, by-status embedding counts, trigger totals, tier breakdown, database size, last indexed time, and startup projection health.
- **Why it matters**: System health must include integrity metrics: embedding backlog, FTS drift, alias divergence, graph orphan rate, storage growth, and projection consistency.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: medium

### Finding 6: The real missing benchmark is compaction continuity
- **Source**: [index.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main/external/src/index.ts), [spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js), [session-bootstrap.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/session-bootstrap.ts)
- **What it does**: Mnemosyne injects memory-tool guidance during `experimental.session.compacting`. Public injects structural transport blocks and records bootstrap duration/completeness, but neither system turns post-compaction recovery into an explicit benchmark.
- **Why it matters**: For long-running agent memory systems, the critical failure mode is often “after compaction, can the agent recover fast and correctly?”
- **Recommendation**: NEW FEATURE
- **Impact**: high

## Assessment
- New information ratio: 0.68
- The external system mostly measures execution success, not memory quality or retrieval accuracy. Public already has the stronger measurement stack.
- The metrics we should track are:
- Memory quality: `confidence`, `validationCount`, `positiveValidationCount`, negative-feedback rate, `access_count`, `last_accessed`, cleanup-candidate rate, archive/unarchive rate.
- Retrieval accuracy: `Recall@K`, channel ablation delta, K-sensitivity, average result confidence, evidence-gap rate, `no_results` / `low_confidence` / `partial` distribution, stage latency breakdown.
- System health: bootstrap duration/completeness, session quality score, graph freshness, embedding status counts, FTS parity drift, alias conflicts, retry stats, database size, startup projection consistency.
- Validation: `bash .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/spec/validate.sh "/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/004-opencode-mnemosyne-main" --strict` returned `RESULT: PASSED` with `Errors: 0  Warnings: 0`, plus the known read-only warning `cannot create temp file for here document: Operation not permitted`.

## Recommended Next Focus
Define a compaction-continuity benchmark for Public: measure recovery success after `experimental.session.compacting`, time to first successful `memory_context` / `memory_search`, and whether scoped/private/shared retrieval remains correct after bootstrap and compaction.
